授業目的: This course examines key features of Canadian business culture, focusing on workplace norms, communication styles, decision-making, and professional expectations. While centred on Canada, the course adopts a comparative perspective, using examples from Japan and other contexts to highlight how cultural differences shape business practices. Through practical cases and discussion, students gain tools for understanding cross-cultural interaction in professional settings.
到達目標: Identify core characteristics of Canadian business culture and workplace norms.Compare Canadian and Japanese approaches to communication, hierarchy, and decision-making.Interpret how cultural expectations influence meetings, teamwork, and professional behaviour.Apply cross-cultural insights to common business situations.Communicate clearly and professionally in a Canadian business context.
授業方法: This course uses interactive lectures, case discussion, and short applied activities. Students analyse real-world business situations, reflect on cultural expectations, and practice professional communication. The Start-up session is delivered on-demand through an instructor video and short online task.

第1回:
Session 1 (Startup Week, 《90 minutes》: Introduction to Canadian Business Culture and course orientation (Professor Dean intro video + short online task) On-demand contents, schedule fixed

Introductory video:https://youtu.be/vlxH06MNZrQ
Short Online Task: See attached file below
第2回: Session 2 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Cultural frameworks and business values in Canada
第3回: Session 3 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Communication styles: directness, politeness, and clarity
第4回: Session 4 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Workplace hierarchy, equality, and informality
第5回: Session 5 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Meetings, participation, and decision-making practices
第6回: Session 6 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Teamwork, collaboration, and individual responsibility
第7回: Session 7 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Professional relationships and networking in Canada
第8回: Session 8 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Business etiquette: emails, interviews, and workplace expectations
第9回: Session 9 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Leadership and management styles in Canadian organizations
第10回: Session 10 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Conflict, feedback, and problem-solving in the workplace
第11回: Session 11 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Work-life balance and organizational culture in Canada
第12回: Session 12 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Comparing Canadian and Japanese business practices
第13回: Session 13 (Core Weeks, 《90 minutes》): Case studies and applied discussion
第14回: Session 14 (Flex Hours, 《90 minutes》): Applied project preparation and consultation
トピック.授業外学習2: Students are expected to complete assigned readings, prepare short reflections or applied tasks, and work on the final project.

教科書/Required texts0: No required textbook. Readings and supporting materials will be provided via the course site.
